name: Trinidad and Tobago

colors.symbolism: the warmth and energy of the sun, the vitality of the land and the courage and friendliness of its people
the sea by which Trinidad and Tobago are bound, the cradle of national heritage, and the equality of all under the sun
the dedication of the people joined together by one strong bond, and the wealth of the land

description: A red field crossed from upper hoist to lower fly by a single broad black diagonal stripe edged with two narrow white fimbriations.
history: Adopted on 31 August 1962, the day Trinidad and Tobago achieved independence from the United Kingdom. The design was created by Carlisle Chang as part of the work of the Independence Committee. The black diagonal across red, fimbriated white, was chosen to represent the nation's three principal elements — earth, water and fire — and is unusual among national flags in using a single thick bend rather than the more common stripe arrangements.

features.symbolism: a single broad black diagonal band fimbriated white, running from the upper hoist to the lower fly across a red field — earth, water and fire; or the past, present and future of the nation
